entry.title: Washington Post: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Police raid kills 18 in Rio, sparking outcry and accusations of abuse
  • Thousands of frogs are dying in Australia. Scientists aren’t sure why.
  • War narrows the many divides between east and west in Ukraine
  • The apocalyptic vision behind Putin’s ‘golden billion’ argument
  • Sri Lanka raids anti-government protest camp as new president takes power
  • Ukraine live briefing: Moscow, Kyiv reach deal on blocked grain, Turkey says; E.U. raises interest rates to tackle inflation
  • Russia’s moves in Africa problematic for U.S. interests, general agrees
  • Italy’s Mario Draghi resigns; new elections are set for September
  • The world’s oldest known male panda, beloved in Hong Kong, dies at 35
  • European Central Bank takes big step to address rising inflation fears
  • In a first, India elects tribal woman to be president
  • Biden: U.S. military does not support Nancy Pelosi visiting Taiwan
  • How Russia is laying the groundwork for its annexation of Ukraine
  • Russia resumes gas flows to Germany after Nord Stream maintenance
  • Ukraine’s first lady says her son, once into arts, now wants to be a soldier
  • Russia and Syria conducted dozens of illegal ‘double tap’ strikes, report says
  • World chess champion to give up title because he’s just ‘not motivated’
  • China fines Didi $1.2 billion for breaking data-security laws
  • Thieves stole ‘Precious Blood’ relic. It reemerged at detective’s door.
  • Methanol found in bodies of 21 teens who died in South African nightclub
  • Deal for Ukraine grain exports due to be sealed in Istanbul
  • A new Libyan force emerges, accused of abusing migrants
  • EXPLAINER: What's behind Europe's spate of deadly wildfires?
  • Deadly raid in Rio favela sparks police violence complaints
  • Russia defends veto of aid to northwest Syria for one year
  • N.Y. GOP governor nominee Zeldin attacked at campaign event, says he’s fine
  • Lightning strike kills one soldier, injures nine at Georgia base
  • A U.K. meteorologist warned of deadly heat. He was told to cheer up.
  • How some TikTokers are explaining the Jan. 6 hearings
  • Woman dressed as nurse and tried to steal baby from hospital, police say
  • Police chief bragged about shooting Black man 119 times, according to recording
  • Trump White House aide goes on sexist tirade, calls Jan. 6 panel ‘anti-White’
  • Video shows California police officer shoot man as he runs away
  • Former Minneapolis officer sentenced to 30 months for violating Floyd’s civil rights
  • Agents protecting Pence feared ‘for their own lives,’ official says
  • ‘I don’t want to say the election’s over.’ Trump dug in after Jan. 6 attack.
  • Trump ‘chose not to act’ as mob terrorized the Capitol, panel shows
  • What we know — and don’t know — about what Trump did on Jan. 6
  • Biden’s bout with covid tests his return-to-normal strategy
  • Officers describe assault on Jan. 6 in front of accused attacker
  • Steve Bannon trial closing arguments set for Friday
  • Post Politics Now: ‘Folks, I’m doing great,’ Biden says after positive coronavirus test
  • Closing arguments in Steve Bannon’s trial set for Friday
  • SEC roils Coinbase insider-trading case by arguing coins are securities
  • Amazon will see you now: Tech giant buys health-care chain for $3.9 billion
  • Zipmex joins growing list of crypto exchanges to block withdrawals
  • Mortgage rates pause ahead of next week’s Federal Reserve meeting
  • A worker was nervous about going on disability. Readers share advice.
  • Tesla dumps Bitcoin amid pressure from factory shutdown
  • Facebook forsakes friends and family to compete with TikTok
  • D.C. family peels away older renovations and unveils dream home
  • Report: Homes deemed unaffordable in most U.S. counties
  • London Can’t Rebrand Its Way to Nasdaq Domination
  • The Populist Experiment in Italy Has Failed
  • Assuming You’ll Retire Healthy Is an Expensive Mistake
  • Macron and Scholz Need a Grand Bargain on Energy
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How Sri Lanka Landed in a Crisis and What It Means: QuickTake
  • Understanding the Unraveling of Tunisia’s Revolution
  • Trump’s behavior on Jan. 6 was worse than you thought. Much worse.
  • China wants to ‘reduce misunderstanding’ with the U.S. It could start by talking.
  • This Jan. 6 hearing was the best showbiz yet. Will it matter? Hmm.
  • Texan politicians won’t say this, but solar is saving their tushies right now
  • In tackling climate change, don’t let the perfect become the enemy of the good
  • Your questions about covid-19, answered by Dr. Leana Wen
  • What do we owe kids for all they sacrificed during covid?
  • Republicans could change government the way they changed the courts
  • Tech giants want to squelch a bill that could give consumers more choice
  • Won’t address climate change for yourselves? How about for the guy who designs heat maps?
  • How Trump violated his oath on Jan. 6 by doing nothing
  • 5 things we learned in the Jan. 6 committee’s stunning hearing
  • This Syrian refugee camp is incubating the next-generation ISIS
  • The Biden administration’s monkeypox response has been a mess
  • Some things Trump might have been doing during the mob attack at the Capitol
  • But their emails
  • Why progressive Democrats are losing ground
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: JoongAng Daily: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Food Ministry to investigate foul smelling Pepsi Zero Sugar bottles
  • DSME, subcontractors reach agreement ending 51-day strike
  • Kia reports strong Q2 performance, net profit up 40% on year
  • Park Ji-hoon to hold fan meet-and-greet events in Japan next month
  • Azerbaijan-Korea relations
  • Loco, Hwasa to collaborate on upcoming EP 'Somebody!'
  • 'Woo' gets mixed reviews from people who know about autism
  • Starbucks Summer Carry Bag may be toxic, company now testing
  • Screenwriter Kim Eun-sook to take part in upcoming Netflix series 'The Glory'
  • Boy band Seventeen to perform latest song '_World' on MTV's 'We Speak Music'
  • Ong Seong-wu, Jo Yu-ri, Enhypen's Jay all test positive for Covid-19
  • ADOR K-pop girl group by Min Hee-jin to be called NewJeans
  • Korea reports third case of Centaurus Covid variant
  • Korea Development Bank puts limit on support as DSME strike continues
  • Samsung Electronics submits plan to invest $192 billion in Texas plants
  • Self-awarding must stop (KOR)
  • Controversy over public viewing rights (KOR)
  • ATP to hold new men's Korea Open in Seoul in September
  • [THINK ENGLISH] 트와이스 9명 전원 JYP 엔터테인먼트와 재계약
  • New Covid-19 cases fall below 70,000
  • No more unauthorized spying
  • Self-awarding must stop
  • Facing free trade’s end
  • Prosecutor general no longer
  • [Today's Cartoon] 2022.07.22
  • Controversy over public viewing rights
  • Nobody wants a garbage incinerator in their back yard
  • Till the last drop
  • A long wait
  • Hanwha Q Cells acquires 66 percent of Germany's Lynqtech
  • Hyundai Oilbank calls off plan to go public, cites market conditions
  • Guessing game begins over Aug. 15 presidential pardons
  • Yoon tax plan designed to help the people and the economy
  • Hyundai Motor's net jumps 55% in second quarter
  • Simultaneous sags in Kospi, Yoon's ratings
  • Spiffy sneaks
  • Posco Holdings' Q2 net profit unchanged from last year
  • UAE embassy in Seoul hosts forum to boost investments
  • Back to work
  • Stocks continue rising as concerns ease over high global energy prices
  • Posco International posts record-highs in Q2 performance
  • Music outlets heap praise on J-Hope's debut solo album 'Jack In The Box'
  • Enhypen’s third EP 'Manifesto : Day 1' tops Japan's Oricon’s daily chart
  • Second case of Centaurus variant detected in Korea
  • Samsung SDI breaks ground on $1.3 billion Malaysian battery plant
  • Anniversary ambassador
  • “Top Gun: Maverick” surpasses 6 million in ticket sales
  • Tax overhaul proposed in Korea as Yoon charts a new course
  • Buy now, pay later gains ground in Korea as marketing tool
  • Comic-Con returns in full force with costumes, crowds
  • National museum introduces cultural innovations of Mesopotamia
  • Viva Republica receives ￦300B in investments this month
  • Hana Bank Seoul E-Prix — what to look out for as Formula E zooms into Jamsil
  • Korea's 'car poor' guys feel like hit-and-run victims
The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: NY Times: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • 5 Takeaways From the Eighth Hearing of the Jan. 6 Committee
  • Why Did It Take So Long to Deploy the National Guard on Jan. 6?
  • Service Service Asked to Stop Investigating Jan. 6 Texts Amid Criminal Inquiry
  • Biden Is Experiencing Mild Symptoms After Testing Positive
  • Why Biden’s Second Booster Wasn’t Enough to Prevent Infection
  • Lee Zeldin, Republican Candidate for N.Y. Governor, Is Attacked at Event
  • Supreme Court Refuses for Now to Restore Biden Plan on Immigration Enforcement
  • Ukraine Tries to Make the Case That It Can Win the War
  • Russia Moves to Close Agency Handling Emigration to Israel
  • House Passes Bill to Ensure Contraception Rights After Dobbs
  • La Guardia, Kennedy and Newark Airports Get a $25 Billion Overhaul
  • 2 Are Indicted in Human Smuggling Case That Left 53 Dead in Texas
  • President Biden Has Covid. Now What?
  • The Future Is Italy, and It’s Bleak
  • We Are Retired Generals and Admirals. Trump’s Actions on Jan. 6 Were a Dereliction of Duty.
  • We’re Asking the Jan. 6 Committee to Do the Impossible
  • Why Republicans Are Having Gas Pains
  • Is Life a Story or a Game?
  • The Shame of Uvalde
  • A Pastor Ripped Apart by Our Divided Country
  • How to Ease Neck and Back Pain From Travel
  • 哈佛招生案和华人的“美国梦”
  • ECB Rates: Europe Joins Inflation Fight With First Increase in 11 Years
  • First Polio Case in Nearly a Decade Is Detected in New York State
  • The Loeb Boathouse in Central Park Will Close This Fall
  • Ukraine News: Turkey Says a Deal Has Been Reached to Unblock Ukrainian Grain Exports
  • Sri Lankan Forces Raid Protest Camp at Center of Uprising
  • Deep in a Covid Wave, Europe Counts Cases and Carries On
  • Italy’s President Accepts Draghi Resignation, Calling for New Elections
  • India’s Next President Will Make History When She’s Sworn In
  • BBC Apologizes to Royal Nanny Swept Up in Diana Reporting Furor
  • Belgium Ratifies Prisoner-Exchange Treaty With Iran
  • Turkish Strike in Northern Iraq Kills at Least 8 Tourists, Officials Say

  • Your Friday Briefing
  • Putin Is ‘Entirely Too Healthy,’ C.I.A. Director Says

  • A Painful Deadline Nears as Colorado River Reservoirs Run Critically Low
  • Risk From Covid-19 Is Increasing in Much of the U.S.
  • In Washington, Olena Zelenska Dressed for Ukraine
  • Australia Avoids Reimposing Covid Rules as Hospitalizations Surge
  • Monarch Butterflies Are Placed on IUCN Red List
  • Russia continues to bombard Kharkiv despite little recent progress in Moscow’s eastern campaign.
  • The Latest Covid Surge and What it Means for the U.S.
  • Ukrainian troops gain confidence after retaking a village.
  • He Built a Home to Survive a Civil War. Tragedy Found Him Anyway.
  • Judge Criticizes Georgia Prosecutor for Aiding the Political Rival of a Trump Ally
  • Ex-Officer Who Held George Floyd’s Legs Sentenced to 30 Months in Prison
  • Video: Josh Hawley Cheered on Jan. 6. Then He Fled.
  • Falling Tree Kills 2 and Injures 3 During Storm in Alabama
  • Outtakes From a Video Show Trump Refusing to Abandon a Lie
  • Melania Trump: ‘I Was Unaware’ of Jan. 6 Violence
  • Who Is Sarah Matthews?
  • Who Is Matthew Pottinger?
  • Jan. 6 Panel Presents Evidence of Trump’s Refusal to Stop the Riot
  • The Hole in the Center of American Politics
  • Is Trump Watching the Jan. 6 Hearings?
  • Train Fire Outside Boston Prompts Evacuation, and a Leap Into the Mystic River

  • Jill Biden Keeps Schedule Despite Close Contact With President

  • What Is the Yield Curve? Wall Street’s Recession Alarm Is Ringing.
  • Russia Restarts Gas Shipments Through Pipeline, but Keeps Germany Guessing
  • Amazon Wants 100,000 Electric Vans. Can Rivian Deliver?

  • Meta tweaks Facebook app to act more like TikTok.

News from the New York Times

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 22 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-22 07:20AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong customs confiscate cannabis worth HK$30 million, arrest suspected triad member
  • Hong Kong government reveals latest line-up of undersecretaries and political assistants, with only 2 deputies to top officials picked from outside administration
  • Stay away from ‘destructive forces’ in industry, John Lee warns city journalists as he urges media to ‘tell Hong Kong stories’
  • Two thirds of mainland customers in Greater Bay Area want to buy insurance products in Hong Kong, survey shows
  • As Hong Kong swelters, offices set thermostats higher to fight climate change, but allow staff to dress down (a little)
  • Hong Kong teenager who kept up studies despite cancer diagnosis among those honoured in Student of the Year award
  • How will Hong Kong respond to crises? Revamped home and youth affairs bureau promises volunteer network, emergency stockpiles for quick community action
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong hospital’s data dashboard cuts patient discharge time by a third
  • China’s Pinduoduo quietly expands e-commerce operations in Hong Kong, wooing consumers with bargains and direct shipping
  • Covid-19 has only reinforced the need for better healthcare in Hong Kong. John Lee must ride the reform wave
  • Hong Kong protests: prosecutors press more serious charges against suspects for carrying zip ties to 2019 clashes
  • Fewer ferries lead to more Chinese white dolphin activity, Hong Kong group finds
  • Secondary schools in Hong Kong close ranks to appeal for class size reduction amid fall in pupil numbers
  • Hong Kong government ‘vehemently condemns’ US lawmakers’ call for sanctions on city’s prosecutors, says it shows disrespect for rule of law
  • Preventing heatstroke: Hong Kong government to include heat index in guidelines for employers to shield outdoor workers from high temperatures
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee tells government department to refund nearly HK$150,000 to district councillor who was charged for putting up 1,214 national flags on October 1
  • Hong Kong consumer price index rises by 1.5 per cent for past 6 months, overall inflation expected to ‘remain moderate in near term’
  • What does 1 degree on the thermostat mean for climate change?
  • Hong Kong police arrest 48 in crackdown on gambling syndicate controlled by triad
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping wishes Joe Biden a speedy recovery from Covid-19
  • China’s focus on gang violence after Tangshan attack does little to protect women
  • Joe Biden schedules summit with African leaders as US renews push to invest in the continent
  • China reporter cyberbullied over Abe tears in reported suicide attempt
  • Ukraine war could deepen China-Russia military ties, Japan defence white paper says
  • Chinese companies to kick off global depositary receipt sales on SIX Swiss Exchange as regulator grants conditional approval for Keda Industrial
  • Australia is risking its own neck for the US in the South China Sea – has it stopped to ask why?
  • Indonesian leader Joko Widodo to meet Xi Jinping in China next week
  • To avoid China-Taiwan military conflict, send Xi clear message on consequences: British spy chief
  • Will South Korea’s Indo-Pacific strategy ‘imitate’ US to counter China’s regional influence?
  • Why China’s Gen Z see climate change as less important than their Western peers
  • Beijing warns US of ‘forceful’ reply to Taiwan visit by Pelosi
  • China smartphone brands such as Xiaomi continue to lead India market despite increased scrutiny, Canalys data shows
  • US scientists keep ties with Chinese peers despite crackdown: researcher
  • EU wants good ties with Beijing, but will still stand with Taiwan, top lawmaker says
  • Tencent to shut down Chinese NFT platform Huanhe a year after launch, report says, amid declining market
  • Chinese scientists unveil high-powered laser that can ‘write’ in the air
  • Two finalists in race for British PM unlikely to lead to big changes in Sino-UK relations
  • China’s banking crisis draws fresh scrutiny as economic slowdown highlights emerging risks
  • Chinese e-cigarette giant gets tobacco production licence, gaining foothold in country’s vaping market
  • China snaps up over US$2 billion of Russian LNG as energy trade bucks economic downturn
  • Baidu’s live-streamed annual tech conference briefly cut off on WeChat service amid China’s tightened online content rules
  • Taiwan: CIA chief warns Beijing looks determined to use force to take the island
  • Taxpayer-funded state funeral for Japan’s Shinzo Abe triggers protests
  • East Timor’s President Jose Ramos-Horta hopes Indonesia will aid ‘symbolic’ Asean entry in 2023
  • Sri Lanka economic crisis: Dinesh Gunawardena becomes new PM as security forces dismantle protest camp
  • India elects Droupadi Murmu as president, its first from a tribal community
  • Thailand confirms first monkeypox infection on day WHO meets to decide global health emergency
  • India’s Sonia Gandhi questioned in historical money laundering case
  • Coronavirus: Tokyo hits pandemic record on rise of new Covid-19 subvariants
  • Will West Sumatra’s new law lead to more Islamic conservatism in Indonesia?
  • ‘Never again, never forget’, torture victim urges amid fears Philippines’ Marcos will repeat father’s human rights atrocities
  • ‘I want to leave Sri Lanka as soon as possible’: thousands jostle for passports to flee economic crisis
  • Biden and Trump: a tale of 2 presidents with Covid-19
  • ‘It’s a massacre’: police raid on Rio favela kills at least 18, sparking anger
  • January 6 panel lays out Donald Trump’s ‘187 minutes of inaction’ when he watched TV in dining room
  • Ukraine war must end to prevent nuclear ‘abyss’, Belarus strongman Lukashenko says
  • Ukraine’s Zelensky hopes to turn tide of war with Russia as US considers fighter jets
  • US records first polio case in almost a decade, and patient has developed paralysis
  • Vladimir Putin and Saudi crown prince talk oil, just days after Joe Biden’s Middle East trip
  • Beloved monarch butterflies now listed as endangered, one step closer to extinction
  • Italy to hold snap election after PM Mario Draghi quits
  • Joe Biden tests positive for Covid-19, ‘very mild symptoms’, White House says
  • BBC pays damages to former royal nanny over ‘false and malicious’ claims when she worked for Princess Diana
  • Vatican’s magnificent art belongs to us say Canada’s indigenous groups
  • Italy’s PM Mario Draghi resigns after coalition falls apart
  • Russia resumes critical gas supplies to Europe via Nord Stream
  • Russian bourse records US$11 million worth of trades in Alibaba, other Hong Kong-listed stocks in first trial month
  • Video: Heatwaves and wildfires engulf parts of US and western Europe
  • Video: Going underground to beat the summer heat in parts of China
  • How emerging market currencies are weathering US dollar surge
  • Hong Kong stocks set for best week in July as China pledges to stem housing rot, Alibaba and Meituan lead tech winners
  • ECB surprises with aggressive hike, first since 2011, to end the era of negative interest rates in eurozone
  • A fifth of MNCs in China rethink office space leasing plans amid tough Covid-19 curbs, while 4 in 100 cancel expansion
  • What does Hong Kong’s move to cut out lawyers in mortgage transactions mean for property buyers
  • Chinese Communist Party branch formed in HSBC onshore venture
  • Central banks forced to continue raising interest rates despite slowdown after months of dithering
  • Britain blocks Chinese company’s deal to acquire camera technology as it could pose risk to national security
  • As China’s mortgage boycott revolt spreads, senior banking regulator assures steps to solve crisis
  • An NFT collection of food or monsters? Artist says it’s both – he finds the ‘beasts’ in mirrored images of dishes, from dumplings to French toast
  • The best dumplings in Hong Kong, a Times Square go-to and a filmmaker’s pick for fried chicken
  • In Disney+ series Pistol, the Sex Pistols, UK punk rock pioneers, are back on TV 45 years on from God Save The Queen
  • His story is also the story of Hong Kong: Alan Chan, designer, on an East-meets-West career
  • 10 years after Psy’s Gangnam Style, has a K-pop song ever hit in the same way?
  • Is your sustainable hotel really ‘net zero’? Almost certainly not – most forget to mention embodied carbon
  • Why you should drink water stored in a copper jug or pot – Ayurvedic experts explain its multiple health benefits, some backed up by science
  • Brazilian wax pros and cons: 5 reasons not to get rid of your pubic hair, and 5 dos and don’ts if you decide to get waxed
  • Instagram and TikTok influencers draw too many tourists, making destinations hard to book
  • On Her Watch: How new materials are revolutionising watchmaking, as Chanel, Rolex, Van Cleef & Arpels, Omega and more brands create exciting women’s timepieces with modern elements and techniques
  • Who is Lalit Modi, the businessman dating Sushmita Sen? The founder of the US$1 billion Indian Premier League just announced on Instagram that he’s in love … but had to flee India in 2010
  • 20 celebrity kids who are BFFs: from Kim Kardashian and Jessica Simpson’s daughters, to J. Lo and Ben Affleck’s teens and Beyoncé’s Blue Ivy and Apple Martin, these stars’ mini-mes are bonding
  • What did Elvis Presley and Caitlyn Jenner have in common? Meet Linda Thompson, the King of Rock ‘n’ Roll’s girlfriend after Priscilla, who married the Kardashians star – but was left out of the biopic
  • Meet Jean Arnault, Bernard Arnault’s youngest LVMH heir: the MIT graduate runs Louis Vuitton watches, did work experience at McLaren Racing, and was inspired by his Tag Heuer CEO brother Frédéric
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • International peace forum to be livestreamed in English for free
  • Experts warn COVID surge not about to subside anytime soon
  • Okinawa invokes special state of emergency for medical system
  • Japan failing to eliminate human trafficking in intern program
  • IT worker combines music instruments with prosthetic arms
  • Tokyo declares ‘red alert’ as it tallies 31,878 patients in a day
  • Panel again does not approve Shionogi’s pill to treat COVID-19
  • State denies failure to offer late Sri Lankan medical care
  • Shiori Ito: Voices of sexual assault victims are being heard at last
  • Insurance policy offered to cover costs for elderly who die alone
  • Head of facility for disabled kids arrested for confining boy
  • Tokyo Olympic organizing exec snared in bribery allegations
  • Tokyo Olympic aftermath still being untangled a year later
  • Alleged Abe killer toyed with bomb attack before opting for gun
  • Medical care close to buckling in some areas in face of 7th wave
  • Cabinet approves state funeral date for slain ex-PM Abe
  • China's BYD to start selling EVs in Japan next year
  • Microsoft Teams back up for most users after global outage
  • Europe's central bank backs larger-than-expected rate hike
  • BOJ retains easy policy, Kuroda shuns chance of near-term rate hike
  • Stockholders seek more female directors among Japanese firms
  • H.I.S. mulls sale of Huis Ten Bosch park to Hong Kong firm
  • Japan trade deficit grows as oil prices surge, yen drops
  • 25% of firms heed directive to let staff work until they reach age 70
  • Tornado kills 1 in eastern China as country faces high temps
  • China’s Didi Global fined $1.2 billion for data violations
  • Baidu unveils latest autonomous electric vehicle: Apollo RT6
  • Taiwan high court allows same-sex marriage of citizen, Japanese
  • Sri Lankan forces make arrests, clear main protest site
  • Myanmar’s economy fragile as fighting, inflation hit poor
  • Heatwave fee for Asia’s gig workers does little to cool climate stress
  • Feds: 1st cryptocurrency insider trading case snares 3
  • Biden tests positive for COVID-19, has 'very mild symptoms'
  • Russia restarts major gas pipeline, expands Ukraine war goals
  • World’s oldest male giant panda dies at age 35 in Hong Kong
  • Biden: Military say a Pelosi Taiwan trip ‘not a good idea’
  • Three long-lost paintings by Taro Okamoto found in Paris
  • TASTE OF LIFE/ Rice bowl dish with egg, Chinese chives and tomato: Life-changing dish served by a neighbor led to a career in cooking
  • Kyoto’s bonfire festival to be held in full for the 1st time in 3 years
  • EDITORIAL: Japan and South Korea off to a good start in mending ties
  • VOX POPULI: When it comes to fruit, bees are the unsung heroes worth protecting
  • VOX POPULI: State funeral for Abe will only serve to extol his actions in life
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Guardian: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live: Ukraine has potential to inflict major losses on Russians, says Zelenskiy; grain export deal ‘to be signed’
  • ‘Growing pains’: researchers question if diagnosis at all useful
  • You be the judge: should my fellow bridesmaid dial down the hen-do plans?
  • Gilberto Gil at 80: ‘Bolsonaro has a retrograde worldview, an opposition to any advance’
  • Being ‘economical with the truth’ has a long history in British politics – but enough is enough | Simon Jenkins
  • England’s gritty win against Spain bodes well for Euro 2022 chances | Karen Carney
  • Anything’s Possible review – Billy Porter’s sweet but uneven trans teen rom-com
  • Weather tracker: record-breaking heat continues to scorch western Europe
  • Acts of Service by Lillian Fishman review – a ‘sex masterpiece’
  • Bronze for Dina Asher-Smith in 200m final as Shericka Jackson goes supersonic
  • Neopets security breach: users’ data reportedly stolen
  • Google Pixel 6a review: this mid-range master is a true bargain
  • No Republican senator supported a climate plan – where is the party on the issue?
  • How US dollars put anti-abortion groups at the heart of Romanian healthcare
  • Japan urges ‘highest level of vigilance’ as Omicron subvariant drives record Covid surge
  • Europe is ablaze, Italian glaciers are collapsing. The climate crisis is here! | First Dog on the Moon
  • House panel says Trump ‘chose not to act’ during attack on US Capitol
  • ‘We have travelled for a month to find grass’: climate crisis piles pressure on Senegal’s herders
  • Sri Lanka security forces raid protest camp as Ranil Wickremesinghe flexes muscles
  • Dozens killed and thousands displaced in election fighting in Papua New Guinea, UN says
  • Labour should be winning – but this Tory leadership circus is drowning Starmer out | Andy Beckett
  • Going underground: inside Ron Howard’s explosive movie about the Thai cave rescue
  • Inside Team Sunak: tech bro vibes and Twixes as No 10 push ramps up
  • ‘Fire ants under my skin’: I was struck down by hell’s itch, the searing pain of the summer
  • Japan sees increasing threat to Taiwan amid Russia’s invasion of Ukraine
  • Hong Kong publishers excluded from book fair over politically sensitive material
  • Splendour in the Grass chaos: first day of music festival cancelled amid wild weather
